one_line_thesis: Block runs Cash App and Square as a dual-sided fintech platform; owner cash ~$3.89/sh (FY2025) with accelerating ex-bitcoin gross profit, but at ~$77 the base return is below our 15% hurdle — watch.

deep_dive.executive_summary:
Block connects 59 million consumer actives and millions of sellers through Cash App and Square. FY2025 operating cash flow was $2.58 billion; after $155 million of capital spending, owner cash is about $3.89 per share. Q1 2026 gross profit grew 27% year over year, led by Cash App (+38%), while Square gross payment volume rose 13%.

At about $77 (Yahoo Finance, 2026-07-09), the Lawrence base case implies about 11.75% per year on normalized owner cash, below our 15% accumulate hurdle. The business has a stable durable advantage (Munger moat) on integrated consumer and seller rails, with parti...

decision.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

valuation.interpretation: value_per_share is withheld while any material additive component is unpriced; priced_components_per_share is not a complete security value.

scenario_contract.rule: Cases must change cited causal operating, capital, probability, timing, or financing drivers—not only a terminal multiple.

change_control.rule: A source fact is locked. Every assumption change requires a reason, author, timestamp, and before/after value.

optionality.rule: Every option requires a beneficiary, probability, timing, remaining capital, failure case, and non-overlap control; absence of option value must be explicit rather than unvalued.

committee.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

method_fit.label: High-return compounder
applicability_tests: Owner earnings can be separated from stock compensation, working capital, and maintenance capital.
Incremental returns on retained capital are observable and durable.
The competitive advantage explains pricing, retention, share, or cost outcomes.
Terminal value is supported by a reinvestment runway rather than an unexplained multiple.
failure_modes: growth projected without its capital cost
terminal value unsupported by a durable mechanism

validation_cohort.purpose: Test depletion, commodity exposure, finite reserves, and royalty-unit reconciliation.
Test tangible capital, credit losses, funding, regulation, and excess-capital realization.
Test owner earnings, capital-light reinvestment, cyclicality, and buyback value creation.
Test normalized margins, industry supply response, replacement cost, and reinvestment timing.
Test product concentration, pipeline probabilities, patent cliffs, and catalyst timing.

method_fit.rule: Use one primary method and no more than two corroborating methods; irrelevant methods and personas remain silent.

outcomes.weighting_rule: Do not change a persona's relevance within a power zone until at least 20 attributable, dividend-aware outcomes exist there; never reweight automatically.

attribution.explanation: A complete security value is intentionally withheld while material components are unpriced. Attribution restarts after the first proof-complete baseline.

total_return_panel.error: Vendor events are discovery evidence; primary-source completeness has not been established.
total_return_panel.return_contract: split-adjusted close excluding dividends; event amounts normalized for later splits; cash reinvested at ex-date close; pre-tax nominal

coverage.note: Vendor events are discovery evidence; primary-source completeness has not been established.

valuation_decision.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

timeline.label: Operating income moved +91.5% versus the prior period (892,327.0 to 1,708,406.0).

Q1 2026: consolidated gross profit +27% year over year; Cash App gross profit +38%; Adjusted EBITDA $1.0B
FY2025 10-K filed: revenue $11.5B (+10%); gross profit $10.4B; Adjusted EBITDA $3.5B (+14%); operating cash flow $2.58B
FY2025 share repurchases 2.5M shares; corporate bitcoin additions continued; bitcoin remeasurement loss $55.9M
FY2024 net income benefited from deferred tax asset release and bitcoin gain $420.9M; normalized earnings lower than GAAP
Ticker changed from SQ to XYZ on NYSE; company brand Block retained with Square and Cash App ecosystems
Afterpay acquisition closed; BNPL integrated into Cash App and Square seller tools
Corporate name changed from Square, Inc. to Block, Inc.; bitcoin treasury strategy formalized
COVID-era Cash App direct deposit and stimulus inflows accelerated consumer actives and engagement
IPO on NYSE as Square, Inc. (SQ); seller payments and small-business software core

industry.structure: U.S. merchant acquiring and SMB software is fragmented but consolidating around integrated platforms (Block, Toast, Fiserv, Adyen). Consumer fintech wallets compete with bank apps, PayPal/Venmo, and Apple Cash on engagement and monetization per active user.
industry.share_shift: Block is gaining gross profit mix toward Cash App (61% of segment gross profit FY2025) while Square GPV grows high-single to low-double digits; bitcoin trading revenue is flat but ex-bitcoin Cash App monetization is accelerating.
industry.trend: Regulatory scrutiny of consumer BNPL and crypto disclosures is rising; AI-assisted seller tools and banking features are the current product battleground; low-margin bitcoin revenue is being de-emphasized in favor of higher-margin financial services.

specialist_mentions.claim: John Ogg beautifully described the theoretical lifecycle of a biotech zombie stock in a 2007 Forbes narrative that is still relevant today.ii XYZ BIOTECH decides to go public with a robust business plan and some initial strong preclinical lab tests.
